items.title: Panic buying prompts PM to reassure Australians over fuel supply
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says nation's supply remains "secure" amid reports of panic buying and shortages.

items.title: Belarus leader gifts rifle to North Korea's Kim as they sign friendship treaty
items.summary: Two key supporters of Russia's war in Ukraine close ranks as Alexander Lukashenko visits Pyongyang.

items.title: Trump's signature to appear on US dollars in first for sitting president
items.summary: The US president's signature will appear on new paper currency alongside Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

items.title: Nepal swears in ex-rapper as new prime minister
items.summary: Balendra Shah won the election by a landslide promising change to Nepalis who are angry at corruption.

items.title: Oscars moving to a new home outside Hollywood
items.summary: The centrally located Peacock Theater in Los Angeles will host the 101st Academy Awards ceremony when it begins streaming on YouTube in 2029.

items.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
items.summary: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

items.title: Sarah Ferguson's 'close Epstein ties' scrutinised in US lawmaker's letter
items.summary: The letter, seen by the BBC, also called on Ferguson to share any knowledge about Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's links to Epstein.

items.title: Whale swims off sandbank after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
items.summary: Rescue teams now hope the whale will swim out of the shallow waters of Lübeck Bay and into the wider sea.

items.title: Spanish woman dies by euthanasia after long legal battle with father
items.summary: The European Court of Human Rights (ECHR) ruled in Noelia Castillo's favour earlier this week.

items.title: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
items.summary: Reports say the US pressured France to rescind Ramaphosa’s invitation after Trump threatened to boycott the summit.

items.title: Judge rejects Pentagon's attempt to 'cripple' Anthropic
items.summary: A federal judge told the government it could not immediately enforce a ban on Anthropic’s tools.

items.title: Orban's Hungarian government accused of mass voter intimidation ahead of election
items.summary: A film quoting voters, mayors and a police officer alleges money and drugs are being offered to pressure people to back the ruling party.

items.title: Singapore denies Malaysian scholar entry, calling her 'undesirable visitor'
items.summary: Fadiah Nadwa Fikri urged some in Singapore to "adopt her brand of radical advocacy", authorities say.

items.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
items.summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.

items.title: How Russia's threat forced Germany to radically rethink its military
items.summary: Germany once terrorised Europe, but now Europe is welcoming a resurgent German military.

items.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
items.summary: The disruption in oil supplies has caused shortages and concerns in several African countries.

items.title: Three charts that are warning signs flashing for Trump on Iran war
items.summary: As gas prices rise, Trump's approval rating is heading into politically dangerous territory.

items.title: The 15-year-old Indian that cricket can no longer ignore
items.summary: At 15, prodigy Vaibhav Suryavanshi is forcing India to ask if he is already ready for the big stage.

items.title: Don't panic - five ways to stop your kids' endless scrolling
items.summary: Parenting experts share their tips on how to keep children's screen time under control.

items.title: 'A game-changing moment for social media' - what next for big tech after landmark addiction verdict?
items.summary: The ruling could be the beginning of the end of social media as we know it, writes the BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman.

items.title: Guthrie on missing mother: 'We cannot be at peace without knowing'
items.summary: In her first interview since the disappearance, Savannah Guthrie recounts the moment she learned her mother was missing and wrestles with the idea that her fame may have made her mother a target.

items.title: What happened in the seconds before Air Canada plane crashed at LaGuardia
items.summary: BBC Verify breaks down the moments before the deadly collision with a fire truck at the New York airport.

items.title: How the deadly LaGuardia Airport crash unfolded
items.summary: Video shows the aftermath of the collision between a passenger plane and a firefighting vehicle on the runway of the New York airport.

items.title: 'Stop, stop, stop': Listen to LaGuardia control tower audio during collision
items.summary: An Air Canada plane carrying 72 passengers and four crew initially landed safely from Montreal on Sunday evening before colliding with a firefighting vehicle.

items.title: Watch: Inside Palestinian homes attacked by settlers
items.summary: Violence began after a teenage settler was killed, reportedly after being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian.
